---
title: "Model Predictions: Jern et al. (2014) Replication"
author: "Victor Moreno"
bibliography: ../references.bib
format: html
---

## Overview
In this script, I define several functions to calculate normative belief trajectories for the experimental paradigm in @jern2014. All analyses are conducted in R [@R2025], following the approaches outlined in @frank2025experimentology.

## Define normative model
```{r}
## Setup
library(tidyverse)
## Prior over the four diseases (less common: L1, L2; more common Y1, Y2)
prior <- c(L1 = 0.01, L2 = 0.35, Y1 = 0.40, Y2 = 0.24)

## P(T = v | V = v)
test_acc <- 0.70   
test_err <- 0.10 ## totals 0.3 across the 3 remaining diseases

## Function to calculate likelihood (Polarization and Moderation conditions)
lik_disease <- function(named) {
  out <- setNames(rep(test_err, 4), names(prior))
  out[named] <- test_acc
  out
}

## Function to calculate likelihood (Control condition)
lik_class <- function(named_class) {
  hi <- 0.70; lo <- 0.30
  if (named_class == "Y") c(L1 = lo, L2 = lo, Y1 = hi, Y2 = hi)
  else c(L1 = hi, L2 = hi, Y1 = lo, Y2 = lo)
}

## Function to calculate posteriors 
posterior <- function(lik1, lik2) {
  unnorm <- prior * lik1 * lik2 ## Calculate raw posterior
  post <- unnorm / sum(unnorm) ## Normalize
  sum(post[c("Y1", "Y2")]) ## Collapse to the more common class
}

## Pooled prior
p_prior <- sum(prior[c("Y1", "Y2")])

## Define predictions 
predictions <- tribble(
  ~Condition, ~p_posterior,
  "Polarization", posterior(lik_disease("Y1"), lik_disease("L1")),
  "Moderation", posterior(lik_disease("Y2"), lik_disease("L2")),
  "Control", posterior(lik_class("Y"), lik_class("L"))
) |>
  mutate(p_prior = p_prior,
         Initial = 200 * p_prior - 100, ## Convert to slider scale 
         Final = 200 * p_posterior - 100)

## Export
modelpredictions <- predictions |>
  pivot_longer(c(Initial, Final), names_to = "Phase", values_to = "Rating") |>
  select(Condition, Phase, Rating)
write_csv(modelpredictions, "../data/processed/JernRep-Results-Full-ModelPredictions.csv")
```

## Define normative model, initialized with participants' actual priors
```{r}
## Set up
df_emmeans <- read_csv('../data/processed/JernRep-Results-Full-MixedModel-EstMeans.csv')

## Pull participants' priors from the emmeans file and convert them to probability scale  
obs_human <- df_emmeans |>
  filter(Phase == "Initial") |>
  mutate(p_obs = (emmean + 100) / 200) |>
  select(Condition, p_obs)

## Unpack participants' priors across the four diseases (emmeans is aggregated across disease classes)
prior_human <- function(p_obs) {
  c(prior[c("L1", "L2")] * (1 - p_obs) / (1 - p_prior),
    prior[c("Y1", "Y2")] * p_obs / p_prior)
}

## Function to calculate posterior
posterior_human <- function(lik1, lik2, prior_vec = prior) {
  unnorm <- prior_vec * lik1 * lik2  ## Calculate raw posterior
  post   <- unnorm / sum(unnorm) ## Normalize 
  sum(post[c("Y1", "Y2")]) ## Collapse to the more common class
}

## Define predictions
predictions_human <- obs_human |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(p_posterior = case_when(
    Condition == "Polarization" ~ posterior_human(lik_disease("Y1"), lik_disease("L1"), prior_human(p_obs)),
    Condition == "Moderation"~ posterior_human(lik_disease("Y2"), lik_disease("L2"), prior_human(p_obs)),
    Condition == "Control" ~ posterior_human(lik_class("Y"), lik_class("L"), prior_human(p_obs))
  )) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(Initial = 200 * p_obs - 100, ## Convert to slider scale
         Final = 200 * p_posterior - 100)


## Export
modelpredictions_human <- predictions_human |>
  pivot_longer(c(Initial, Final), names_to = "Phase", values_to = "Rating") |>
  select(Condition, Phase, Rating)
write_csv(modelpredictions_human, "../data/processed/JernRep-Results-Full-ModelPredictions-HumanPrior.csv")

```
